public_goals: Avoid surprise reimbursement requests long after an expense was incurred.
Receive receipts and basic documentation before repayment is expected.
Use a predictable process for non-routine child expenses.
private_concerns: Feels reimbursement requests are sometimes raised only after resentment has already built up.
Worries vague expense requests invite repeat conflict.
Does not want asking for receipts to be treated as hostility.
red_lines: Will not accept repeated reimbursement demands without supporting receipts.
Will not accept non-routine expenses being treated as automatic obligations without notice.
emotional_triggers: surprise reimbursement requests after long delays
disclosure_tendencies: becomes more specific when timelines and receipts are discussed concretely
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: gets more defensive if asked to pay without documentation
becomes more open when the process distinguishes fair reimbursement from surprise demands
starting_positions: Primary position: reimbursement requests should include timely notice and supporting receipts.
Secondary position: non-routine expenses should not be treated as payable without a clear process.
likely_openings: I should not be getting surprise reimbursement requests weeks later.
If I am expected to pay something, I need receipts and a clear timeline.
